headline: Partners reclaim 45 hrs/week from contract review
summary: IDP layer pre-reads every clause that deviates from firm standards. Partners only review exceptions.
challenge: Contract review consumed 32 hours per partner per week. The firm tried two contract-management SaaS tools and an internal automation team. Neither moved the needle — partners still had to read every clause to spot deviations from the firm's standards.
approach: We deployed an Intelligent Document Processing layer trained on the firm's historical contracts. It pre-reads every incoming agreement, classifies clauses, and flags only those that deviate from the standards. Partners review the exceptions — typically 8–12% of total clauses — instead of every line.
result: In week 3 the firm hit ROI. By month 2 partner time on contract review dropped 78%. The reclaimed 45 hrs/week per partner now goes to client-facing work, which has lifted billable revenue 22% over the same period.
quote: Aiprosol pulled 45 hours a week out of partner time inside three weeks. The IDP layer paid for itself before we even finished onboarding.

headline: Lead response 6hr → 3min, +28% conversion
summary: Instant AI qualifier responds, qualifies, books showings before competitors call back.
challenge: Average lead response time was 6 hours. By the time the agent rang back, most prospects had already booked a viewing with a competitor. The agency had tried hiring an SDR, but the cost was prohibitive against the conversion lift.
approach: An AI qualifier that responds within 60 seconds, asks the right qualifying questions, and books a viewing on the assigned agent's Calendly — all before the agent even knows the lead exists. Edge cases route to a human within the same channel.
result: Response time dropped from 6 hours to under 3 minutes — a step-change, not a 30% improvement. Conversion lifted 28% within 2 weeks. The agency hasn't hired a single new SDR; the AI handles the volume of 2.5 reps.
quote: Lead response was a known weak spot. Arora's qualifier dropped it from 6 hours to under 3 minutes. Conversion is up 28% and we haven't hired a single new SDR.

headline: Defect rate 4.1% → 0.6% with vision + telemetry
summary: Real-time anomaly flagging routes parts to a quality engineer before they ship.
challenge: After a decade of process work, defect rate plateaued at 4.1%. Every defective unit shipped was a customer escalation, a return, and an internal RCA. The QC team was reactive — discovering issues days after they originated.
approach: A vision + telemetry pipeline at the line level: cameras flag visual anomalies in real time, sensors detect subtle drift in machine output, both feed an anomaly model that routes alerts to a quality engineer the moment a part exits the line. No part ships without passing this gate.
result: Defect rate dropped to 0.6% within 8 weeks of deployment. Throughput rose 34% because the line stops less often for downstream rework. Customer escalations dropped 81% in the same window.
quote: We've tried three automation consultancies. Aiprosol is the only one that delivered a roadmap with real numbers attached and then actually hit them.

headline: Stockouts -71%, $95k annual saving
summary: POS + supplier + forecast data fused into a demand-prediction model that auto-orders 3 weeks ahead.
challenge: Stockouts cost roughly $12k/month in lost sales across 14 stores. Manual ordering relied on a buyer's feel for demand, and supplier lead times were unpredictable. Excess stock on slow movers tied up another $40k of working capital.
approach: POS data, supplier lead-time history, and an external demand-signal feed (weather, events, calendar) feed a forecasting model that orders 3 weeks ahead of need per SKU per store. Buyers approve in batches; the system handles the routine 80%.
result: Stockouts fell 71%. Excess stock on slow movers fell 38%, releasing $52k of working capital. Combined annual saving in year 1: $95k against a build cost recouped inside 9 weeks.
quote: The forecast accuracy is what surprised us most. We expected automation; we got a buyer that out-performs ours on the routine SKUs.

headline: Lead-gen playbook paid back in week 1, then upgraded to Growth
summary: Self-serve playbook to managed plan in 6 weeks. Now running 4 automations with sub-3-min response on inbound.
challenge: Three-person agency drowning in admin. Founder Priya doing client work + sales + ops. Inbound leads sat in a single shared inbox for 24-48 hours before anyone got to them. Lost deals were piling up.
approach: Started with the $127 Lead Generation Automation Playbook — Priya self-implemented over a weekend. Inbound qualifier + CRM sync went live in 3 days. Within 5 weeks she upgraded to Growth ($2,997/mo) for sales sequencing, document processing, and reporting automation.
result: Lead response dropped from 24-48hr to under 3 minutes. Conversion lifted 31%. Founder reclaimed ~12 hrs/week. The playbook ROI alone was 800% in the first month; the Growth plan ROI is on track for 420% in year 1.
quote: The $127 playbook alone made us our money back in week one. We then upgraded to Growth and never looked back.

headline: Delivery dispatch automated, ops time -54%
summary: Dispatcher AI takes inbound orders, allocates to the right driver in real time, replaces a 3-person ops desk.
challenge: A regional logistics firm with 40+ drivers ran dispatch out of a 3-person ops room. Operators stitched together orders from 5 different customer portals, allocated by gut feel, then radioed drivers. Order-to-dispatch lag was 18-30 minutes; delivery hit-rate was 76%.
approach: Multi-channel order ingestion (email, EDI, REST APIs, web portals) into a unified queue. AI dispatcher allocates based on driver location, route load, customer SLA, and traffic. Drivers see jobs on a mobile app within 2 minutes of order receipt. Ops staff oversee, override exceptions, handle customer escalations.
result: Dispatch time dropped from 18-30 min to under 4 min. Hit-rate climbed to 94% in 11 weeks. Ops time -54% — two of three roles repurposed to customer success. Annual cost saving: $128k against a build cost recouped in 14 weeks.
quote: We expected automation; we got a virtual ops manager that doesn't sleep. The dispatch decisions are at least as good as our best human.

headline: Candidate screen time -82%, placement velocity 2.3×
summary: AI candidate screener pre-qualifies applications, drafts shortlists, schedules interviews — recruiters do the actual recruiting.
challenge: Mid-market recruiting firm processing 1200+ applications/month across 15 active roles. Recruiters spent 60% of their time reading CVs, drafting screening emails, and scheduling first calls. Time-to-fill averaged 47 days against an industry benchmark of 30.
approach: AI screener reads every application against the role's ICP. High-confidence matches (top 15%) auto-progress to the screening sequence — personalised email, calendar invite, intake form. Borderline cases (next 25%) ranked and routed to a recruiter. The bottom 60% get a polite, branded rejection within 90 minutes.
result: Recruiter screening time dropped 82%. Time-to-fill improved from 47 days to 20. Placement velocity 2.3× — the firm filled 73 roles in the first quarter post-deployment vs 32 the prior quarter. Candidate NPS jumped because rejections came fast.
quote: Arora as the AI CEO is the best decision we made this year. She handles the routine 80% so my team can focus on the 20% that actually moves revenue.
